name: Checks Originals
description: This artwork may or may not be notable.
about:
## This artwork may or may not be notable

Checks is an artwork by Jack Butcher, born from a cultural flashpoint: when Elon Musk transformed Twitter's blue verification checkmark from a marker of institutional legitimacy into a purchasable subscription. The artwork — a grid of colorful checkmarks — asks what happens when anyone can buy the symbol of notability. Launched as a 24-hour open edition on January 3, 2023, at $8 per mint — a direct reference to the cost of Twitter Blue verification — 16,031 Checks Edition pieces were minted. Each piece consists of 80 checks arranged in an 8×10 grid.

## Fully onchain art

The Checks Originals smart contracts were designed and developed by Jalil Wahdatehagh to transform Checks into a fully onchain generative artwork. Unlike most NFT projects that store images on IPFS or centralized servers, every Checks Original is rendered as SVG directly from the smart contract. The artwork, its colors, and its metadata are all computed and stored onchain with no external dependencies. Editions can be migrated to Originals at any time — migration burns the Edition token and mints an Original that retains the same token ID, removing artist control and making the work fully trustless.

## Compositing and the burn ladder

The number 80 is semi-perfect — its proper divisors (1, 4, 5, 10, 20, 40) sum to exactly 80. This mathematical property structures the entire compositing system. Holders can burn two tokens of one tier to produce one token of the next tier: two 80-check pieces become one 40-check, two 40s become a 20, and so on down to a single check. The ultimate prize — a Black Check — requires burning 64 single-check tokens, which means 4,096 original editions must be consumed to create one. Given the total supply of 16,031, at most 3 Black Checks can ever exist.
risks: Compositing and burning are irreversible — burned tokens are permanently destroyed
Black Check creation requires burning 4,096 original editions — significant capital at risk
Onchain SVG rendering depends on contract storage — future hard forks could theoretically affect rendering

mint.title: Mint Original
mint.description: Mint Checks Originals by burning the corresponding Edition tokens. Each Original is created fully onchain with the same token ID as the burned Edition. This is the only way new Originals enter existence.

tokenIds.label: edition token IDs
tokenIds.description: The Edition token IDs to mint

getCheck.title: Get Check
getCheck.description: Look up the full onchain data for a specific Check, including its visual genome and compositing history.

tokenId.label: token ID
tokenId.description: The Check token ID (matches the original edition number)

svg.title: Render SVG
svg.description: Render the fully onchain SVG artwork for a Check. The entire image is generated from contract storage.

colors.title: Colors
colors.description: Get the color names and values used in a specific Check.

tokenURI.title: Token URI
tokenURI.description: Returns the full onchain metadata and base64-encoded SVG for a Check. No external hosting or IPFS dependency.

composite.title: Composite
composite.description: Burn two Checks of the same tier to create one Check of the next tier (e.g. two 80-checks become one 40-check). The kept token ID carries forward.
composite.warning: Permanently burns one token. The burn cannot be reversed.

tokenId.label: keep token ID
tokenId.description: The token ID to keep (carries forward)

swap.label: swap visual
swap.description: By default, the kept token's colors carry into the composite. Enable swap to use the burned token's colors instead — the kept token ID survives, but it looks like the burned one

compositeMany.title: Composite Many
compositeMany.description: Batch-composite multiple pairs of Checks in a single transaction. Each pair burns one token and keeps the other.
compositeMany.warning: Permanently burns tokens. Burns cannot be reversed.

burnIds.label: burn token IDs
burnIds.description: The token IDs to burn (matched by index with keep IDs)

simulateComposite.title: Simulate Composite
simulateComposite.description: Preview what a composite would produce without executing it. Returns the resulting Check data.

simulateCompositeSVG.title: Simulate Composite SVG
simulateCompositeSVG.description: Preview the SVG artwork that would result from compositing two Checks, without executing the burn.

infinity.title: Infinity (Black Check)
infinity.description: Burn 64 single-check tokens to create a Black Check — the rarest possible outcome. Given the original supply of 16,031, at most 3 could ever be created.
infinity.intent: Burn 64 single-check tokens to create a Black Check
infinity.warning: Irreversibly burns 64 single-check tokens. Requires all tokens to be single-checks.

inItForTheArt.title: Sacrifice
inItForTheArt.description: Sacrifice one Check to transfer its visual identity to another. Burns the sacrificed token and overwrites the receiving token's visual genome.
inItForTheArt.intent: Sacrifice Check #{burnId} to transfer its visual to #{tokenId}

tokenId.label: receiving token ID
tokenId.description: The token that receives the visual

burnId.label: sacrificed token ID
burnId.description: The token to burn (its visual transfers to the receiver)

inItForTheArts.title: Sacrifice Many
inItForTheArts.description: Batch-sacrifice multiple Checks in a single transaction.

burn.title: Burn
burn.description: Permanently burn a Check. The token is destroyed and removed from supply.
burn.warning: Irreversible — the token is permanently destroyed.

resolveEpochIfNecessary.title: Resolve Epoch
resolveEpochIfNecessary.description: Resolves the current epoch using a commit-reveal scheme. Epochs seed the randomness that determines a token's visual traits upon migration. Called automatically when needed but can be triggered manually.

getEpochData.title: Epoch Data
getEpochData.description: Returns data for a specific epoch, including the reveal block used for randomness.

totalSupply.title: Total Supply
totalSupply.description: Current number of Checks Originals in existence (minted minus burned).

Composite.description: Emitted when two Checks are composited, burning one and producing a piece with fewer checks (e.g. 80 → 40).

Sacrifice.description: Emitted when a Check is sacrificed to transfer its visual identity to another.

Infinity.description: Emitted when 64 single-check tokens are burned to create a Black Check.

NewEpoch.description: Emitted when a new epoch is created for the commit-reveal randomness scheme.

MetadataUpdate.description: Emitted when a token's metadata changes (e.g. after compositing).
